themes.corpus_position: Basal cell carcinoma is the most common type of skin cancer and usually presents as a shiny pimple that doesn't heal. When in doubt, get it checked out.

themes.name: Glass skin — her win + civilian wave
themes.receipt: 3 breakouts · 2 creators (incl. HER, both platforms) · 3.4M views · 7–13s winning · glassskin-seeded stitch pool is deep

videos.author: drabby6
videos.caption: And don’t forget to exfoliate 1-2 times a week! Don’t use a retinoid when pregnant. What's your glass skin care routine? #glassskin #skincare #skincareroutine

videos.author: abby.waldmanmd
videos.caption: And don’t forget to exfoliate 1-2 times a week! Don’t use retinoids when pregnant. other retinoids like retinol or adapalene can be used instead of tretinoin Azelaic acid can be used at night instead

videos.author: teawithmd
videos.caption: If ✨ glass skin✨ is what you want, why aren’t you using this combo yet!? And no it’s not just genetics - I suffer from hormonal acne and rosacea! #azelaicacid #tretinoin #glassskin

her_coverage.summary: the wave's biggest video IS hers — TT superviral 1.6M (♥ gold, top-1% eng) + IG 1.3M (♥ silver), posted Tue 7/7; trifecta prequel 12.6d

themes.corpus_position: Trifecta for glass skin: azelaic acid + tretinoin + zinc-oxide diaper cream for barrier; exfoliate 1–2×/week; don't use a retinoid when pregnant.

themes.name: Sun-protection habits beat products (peak summer)
themes.receipt: 4 breakouts · 4 creators · 3.7M views · 6–12s gags to 60–160s explainers · anti-sunscreen misinformation wave still live underneath

videos.author: beautydermmd
videos.caption: Not her serum. Not her skincare. HER HABITS. 👀 Sun protection is elite behavior. UPF clothing. Wide-brim hat. Sunglasses. Shade. This is what dermatologists notice first. You should too. ☀️ #dermatolo

videos.author: drdrayzday
videos.caption: Diaper rash cream is not sunscreen. Always look for sunscreens that are labeled broad spectrum and with a stated SPF value (ideally 30 or higher). Don’t assume the presence of high concentrations of z

videos.author: derm.talk
videos.caption: Comment “UPF” and I’ll send you items in this video. Looks extra… because it is. But this is exactly how I dress after a laser or procedure. 😂 #upfclothing #sunprotection #derm #skincarehacks #skincar

her_coverage.summary: sunscreen-myths video posted ~1d (TT 24K + IG 50K, climbing); UV-index reminder + protective-clothing takes in corpus (Apr 2024)

themes.corpus_position: Sunscreen doesn't cause cancer. If you're going to be out, wear protective clothing — large wide-brim hat, long sleeves; UV-protective clothing is even better than regular cloth, but any sort of protection is great.

stitch_shortlist.author: @tierra5239
stitch_shortlist.claim: cooling sleeves protect skin from sun damage

pitch.the_claim: arm 'cooling sleeves' double as sun protection for summer outings
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.6M in 4.1d on a product civilians already own; half-right — sleeves only block UV reliably when UPF-rated; promo-flavored source (doubt stated), so the correction carries real value
pitch.your_fit: verbatim corpus (Apr 2024 + anti-aging guide): 'UV-protective clothing is even better than regular cloth — but any sort of protection is great'
angles: yes-and: love the sleeves — here's the UPF label to check first
test-style: UPF-rated vs random sleeve, what actually blocks
her Mohs-surgeon summer uniform in 15s

pitch.the_claim: the leg rash after long hot theme-park days is 'Disney rash' — exercise-induced vasculitis, temporary
pitch.why_it_stitches: 733K in 3.5d, pacing 209K/day (guarantee pace); media explainer punching sideways; peak vacation season — comments full of 'this happened to me'
pitch.your_fit: adjacent authority: vasculitis ('inflammation of the blood vessels') covered in her Feb 2026 '10 skin warning signs' video; no Disney-rash-specific video — lane open
angles: name it + calm it: why heat + long walks does this to legs
when it's NOT harmless: warning signs from her Feb video
theme-park week prevention: what works, what's myth

pitch.the_claim: everyone should double-shampoo — first wash breaks down buildup, second cleans
pitch.why_it_stitches: 382K on a concrete, correctable routine claim in her most underexploited lane (scalp/hair); 5.9d so past peak — recorded as the rubric miss
pitch.your_fit: no corpus position on double-shampooing — scalp is her named high-ceiling gap; flag: needs her take before drafting
angles: needs her take: who benefits from a double cleanse — who's just stripping their scalp
oily vs dry scalp: one-size washing advice is the real myth

stitch_shortlist.author: @ericakisler
stitch_shortlist.claim: something made my skin cancer surface faster

pitch.the_claim: patient believes years of sun set up her skin cancer but a recent trigger made it 'come to the surface faster'
pitch.why_it_stitches: 558 comments on 228K views — disproportionate confusion to answer; patient story = her strongest react pattern; empathy-first only (punch nothing); misses recorded: 6.4d old, sub-500K
pitch.your_fit: core corpus: UV damage is cumulative over decades — 'the best way to prevent skin cancer is to protect yourself from UV' (2023 explainers + BCC guide 493K); a Mohs surgeon is exactly whom patients ask 'why now?'
angles: empathy-first: her question is the most common one in clinic
'did X cause my skin cancer?' — what actually sets the timeline
what a Mohs surgeon tells every patient who asks 'why now'

stitch_shortlist.author: @olympiaanley
stitch_shortlist.claim: conventional sunscreens are 'dozens of toxic chemicals'

pitch.the_claim: most conventional sunscreens are toxic (oxybenzone, octocrylene…) and should be avoided
pitch.why_it_stitches: the exact claim she myth-busted ~1d ago — the wave is still live (late-June '292% melanoma risk' study misread, since debunked by Snopes/TheJournal fact-checks); 38K/day and climbing; miss recorded: 141K views
pitch.your_fit: her own video ~1d ago: 'Sunscreen doesn't cause cancer — the myth evolves from evidence that some chemical sunscreens absorb…'; playbook: broad-spectrum SPF 30+, tinted for melasma. Repeat topic → needs the NEW react angle, not a rerun
angles: react-format: play the claim, answer with her 1d-old myth-bust + receipts
the olive branch: worried? mineral-only is fine — here's how to pick
'read the study with me': the 292% misread in 30s

stitch_shortlist.author: @labmuffinbeautyscience
stitch_shortlist.claim: Dr Dray is right in the diaper-cream sunscreen debate

pitch.the_claim: in the derm-vs-derm debate, zinc-oxide % means diaper cream can't stand in for sunscreen
pitch.why_it_stitches: active derm-vs-derm debate (Dr Dray, science creator labmuffin); @drdrayzday's own diaper-cream video is a 3% ♥ gold breakout in THIS week's feed — the argument has legs; miss recorded: 50K views; fellow experts correct → 'yes, and' only
pitch.your_fit: she's IN this one: her glass-skin trifecta includes zinc-oxide diaper cream — as BARRIER, not SPF (corpus: 'use a zinc-based barrier… everyday diaper cream', tretinoin + microneedling guides)
angles: 'yes, and': diaper cream is in my trifecta — here's what it does and doesn't do
barrier ≠ broad-spectrum: the label difference in 20s

news.headline: FDA accepts roflumilast cream sNDA for infant eczema
news.summary: First steroid-free topical PDE4 could reach infants 3–24 months (~1M US kids); decision due Feb 23, 2027 — a parent-question magnet for her audience
news.her_angle: what eczema parents should ask before assuming steroids are the only option
